sections.title: Pre-quest
Attempting to climb up the stairs

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! Come out here. We need to talk!

sections.title: Talking to Henryeta Carnillean

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: How could this happen? How? Will our pain never end?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Leave me at once! I don't want to talk right now.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe Carnillean

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: It's not fair! All the bad things keep happening to us! Why don't they happen to anyone else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's happened?

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I shouldn't talk to you. You might be one of the bad people.

sections.title: Talking to Claus the Chef

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: This poor family. I don't know how much more they can take.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Sorry, I shouldn't be talking to you about it.

sections.title: A Death in the Family
Talking to the Carnillean guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hey! I need to talk to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, you. I have some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You were recently hired by Sir Ceril Carnillean, correct?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right... he wanted me to find some cultists that stole his armour.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: And were you successful?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: And have you had any contact with the Carnilleans since your success?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: And have you had any contact with the Carnilleans since your failure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I'm very confused. What's all this about?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: An unthinkable crime has taken place, and as someone who has recently had contact with the Carnilleans, you are one of our suspects.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A crime? What's happened?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Until I am satisfied that you were not involved, I can't say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then what do I need to do to convince you I wasn't?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sorry about all this. I'm pretty confident you had nothing to do with any of it, but I need to put on a good show for Lady Carnillean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well I must say adventurer, that is quite the alibi. You clearly had no involvement in this horrific crime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm glad we could...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: And what's that? You want to help us find the culprit? How generous of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I never said...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! I would never sit by while criminals are on the loose! You will have all the help you need!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent! Please join me upstairs when you're ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor have I just agreed to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I need to get going.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: But...

sections.title: Inspecting Sir Ceril Carnillean before talking to the Guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Let me run you through what happened before you start inspecting things.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard upstairs
Talking to the guard again

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Are you done looking around the crime scene?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Not to worry. Take all the time you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that...?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Sir Ceril Carnillean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I was hoping you could help work that out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I guess we should get started right away. What do we know so far?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: It happened in the early hours of the morning. According to Lady Carnillean, Sir Ceril heard a noise coming from Butler Jones' old room. He got up to investigate.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: After a few moments, she heard Sir Ceril shout something, followed by a scream. She went to see what was happening, and this is what she found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So no one else was in the room?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Not that she saw.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where was everyone else at this point?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I was the guard on duty last night. I was stationed out front as usual. Claus, the family chef, was down in the kitchen preparing an early breakfast for Sir Ceril.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Young Philipe was in bed. He slept through the entire thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do we have any obvious suspects?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Jones is behind bars, but many of his fellow cultists are still at large. Perhaps this was an act of revenge on their part.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: It happened in the early hours of the morning. According to Lady Carnillean, Sir Ceril heard a noise coming from Butler Jones' room. He got up to investigate.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: After a few moments, she heard Sir Ceril shout something, followed by a scream. She went to see what was happening, and this is what she found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So no one else was in the room?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Not that she saw.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where was everyone else at this point?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I was the guard on duty last night. I was stationed out front as usual. Claus, the family chef, was down in the kitchen preparing an early breakfast for Sir Ceril.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Young Philipe was in bed. He slept through the entire thing. Jones himself was downstairs at the time, but he vanished before we could question him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jones vanished?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Indeed. That, along with the murder occurring in his room, makes him a prime suspect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apart from Jones, do we have any other suspects?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: We also have those cultists that broke in a while back. Following his disappearance, we can't help but wonder if Jones might have been in league with them...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can't rule anything out.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: There's a few other things to note, but it's probably best if you have a look around the crime scene before we go any further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea. I'll begin at once.

sections.title: Talking to Henryeta Carnillean after learning of the death

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: My dear husband... how could this happen? Will our pain never end?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry for your loss.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Please, leave me. I would mourn alone.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe Carnillean after learning of the death

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: It's not fair! All the bad things keep happening to us! Why don't they happen to anyone else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: Then you're useless! Leave me alone!

sections.title: Talking to Claus the Chef after learning of the death

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: I still don't believe it. This poor family. I don't know how much more they can take.

sections.title: Inspecting Sir Ceril's corpse

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've seen everything I need to. I should check in with the guard.

sections.title: Inspecting the broken window

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've seen everything I need to. I should check in with the guard.

sections.title: Knocking at the false wall
Inspecting the chest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... was someone after Hazeel's restoration scroll?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've seen everything I need to. I should check in with the guard.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after inspecting the crime scene

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Are you done looking around the crime scene?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Find anything of note?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that's everything.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: So someone broke in from the outside, something may have been stolen from a hidden room upstairs, and Sir Ceril was stabbed in the back. All useful information, but none of it gives us anything definitive.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Thankfully, we do have one more lead that might hold some promise. Take a look at this.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I found this by the window. That skull on it is the symbol of the Khazard Army. There's also a name engraved into the back: 'Evelot'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like we need to find this Evelot and see what they know.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: While I agree, my superiors won't be happy if I open an investigation into a member of the Khazard Army. I think you'll need to handle this bit yourself. Besides, I need to take care of matters here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: If you want a place to start, I'd suggest the bar at the Fight Arena. Places like that are always good for gathering information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should we not look into the Hazeel Cult as well?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: They're definitely top suspects, but we don't have any actual evidence linking them to the crime. Until we do, this Evelot lead seems like a safer bet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why can't you investigate the Khazard Army yourself?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: The matter is... complicated. On paper, General Khazard is an upstanding citizen, and his private army operates with approval from the crown. King Lathas even hired them for some peacekeeping once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: It's fair to say that Khazard is a bit... controversial. His war with the gnomes is creating more than a few problems for Kandarin, not to mention the rumours about the people fighting in his arena...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: For now though, King Lathas considers him a valuable ally. As such, it is preferred we maintain a good relationship with him and his army.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Unlike his predecessor, King Thoros is keen to slowly distance himself from Khazard. However, in the meantime, we have to maintain a good relationship with him and his army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's where I come in. I understand.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: No need to worry. Some of our best are already out searching for him. I'm sure he won't get far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could help them?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Your concern is appreciated, but they'll be fine. You're better off focusing on Evelot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The window was broken from the outside.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I didn't see anyone leave or enter from the front of the building last night, so that makes sense. The window must have been their way in and out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it was likely someone who didn't have any other way to access the mansion. We can probably rule out staff and family members.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Agreed. Did you find anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril was stabbed from behind.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: So he wasn't facing his killer. But if that were the case, why did Lady Carnillean say she heard him shouting? Surely he'd have been facing whoever he was shouting at.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That could mean there were two people in the room with him.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Let's hope not. One killer on the loose is bad enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something else as well. It looks like the blade used to kill him was unusually thin.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Really? How odd. I'll make a note to look into that once we're done here.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Anyway, did you find anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A hidden room above us was broken into.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: A hidden room? What hidden room?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks like it can only be accessed from in here. It doesn't seem like anyone has been in there for some time.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What's in this room?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a chest up there that's been broken into.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Do you think they took anything from it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know. I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: This raises more questions than answers. Was this actually a murder, or just a robbery gone wrong? And how was there a hidden room that we knew nothing about?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Did you find anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to go.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh. Well don't be gone too long. We have work to do here.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after telling him the three options

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: So did you find anything of note in your investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've gone over everything.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: So someone broke in from the outside, something may have been stolen from a hidden room upstairs, and Sir Ceril was stabbed in the back. All useful information, but none of it gives us anything definitive.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after saying you have gone over everything

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: So someone broke in from the outside, something may have been stolen from a hidden room upstairs, and Sir Ceril was stabbed in the back. All useful information, but none of it gives us anything definitive.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after he mentions the bar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. You should head over to the bar at the Fight Arena and see what you can learn about this Evelot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

sections.title: On the Trail
Talking to the Khazard Barman

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Hi, what can I get you? We have a range of quality brews.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anyone called Evelot?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for them.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: It's not my place to go giving out information to people I don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? I could make it worth your while.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Are you suggesting that I'm the kind of person that would besmirch my honour for a measly bribe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Because you'd be absolutely right! 100 coins will pay for the information you desire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, here's the money.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can you tell me about Evelot?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: So oddly enough, she was in here not that long ago, but just before you arrived, one of the lads came in and spoke to her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To say what?

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: No idea, but she left in quite the hurry afterwards. Perhaps you'll be able to pick up her trail if you look around outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Fancy a drink before you go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll come back when I have the money.

lines.speaker: Khazard Barman
lines.text: Fancy a drink before you go?

lines.speaker: Player

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after talking to the Barman

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck at the Fight Arena?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Keep at it.

sections.title: Talking to Evelot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Evelot, I presume?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: If you think I'm coming quietly, you've got another thing coming!

sections.title: Running away from Evelot
Talking to Evelot after defeating her
Talking to Evelot again

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I thought you were going to see that cult?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will be soon.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Well I've told you everything I know, so no reason for you to still be lurking around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're sure.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Alright, that's enough! You win!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So will you answer my questions now?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: It doesn't seem like I have any other choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ceril Carnillean has been murdered, and an amulet belonging to you was found at the scene of the crime.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: What? My amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, quite sloppy of you.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Don't patronise me! Do you really think I'd be so foolish as to leave my own amulet at the scene of a murder? The amulet was stolen a few days ago. Someone must have planted it there to set me up!

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I knew I shouldn't have taken this job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Job? What job?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I needed money, badly. Some guy hired me for a simple robbery. Nice and straightforward, he said. All I had to do was break into that Carnillean house and retrieve something for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who was this person?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I don't know. He kept his face hidden. All I know is that he was willing to pay a lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what did he want you to steal?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Just some random scroll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened to Ceril?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: That had nothing to do with me! I admit, he saw me on my way out, but I was able to escape. I only found out he was dead after I got back to the Fight Arena!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... What about this scroll? What was it?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: No idea. I was told to deliver it to some cultists south of the city, which I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so it was the Hazeel Cult that hired you. That's not surprising.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: No, that's the thing. They were very happy when I gave them the scroll, but they had no idea I'd been hired to retrieve it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But if that's the case, then who hired you?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I told you, I don't know! He was meant to meet me in the bar earlier to pay me, but he never showed up. This is such a mess!

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Just some random scroll, but it wasn't even there! The whole thing was for nothing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened to Ceril?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: That had nothing to do with me! I admit, he saw me on my way out, but I was able to escape. I only found out he was dead after I got back to the Fight Arena!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Say the scroll had been there, what were you supposed to do with it?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I was told to deliver it to some cultists south of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But that doesn't make sense. The reason the scroll wasn't there is because the Hazeel Cult already managed to steal it. Why would they hire you to steal it again?

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: I told you, I don't even know who hired me! The guy was meant to meet me in the bar earlier to pay me, but he never showed up. This is such a mess!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't like this. Something very strange is going on here. I need to find this person that hired you.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Good luck with that. He didn't seem like the kind of person who'd want to be found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I guess my best bet is to pay a visit to the Hazeel Cult. They're involved in some way, even if they themselves don't realise it.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: So am I free to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't stop you, but you should consider going to the authorities. They might be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Evelot
lines.text: Ha! Nice joke. I'm better off on my own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're sure. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after the conversation with Evelot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck at the Fight Arena?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. I have a new lead to investigate.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well, let me know once you have something.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel/Alomone

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Well, well, well, what do we have here?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Lord Hazeel, this is the adventurer who killed Alomone and got Jones captured.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: I see. So it was your actions that prevented my followers from bringing about my restoration?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I guess so.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: There was once a time when any who dared oppose me would flee to the furthest corners of this world to escape my wrath. Alas, it seems that in my absence, you mortals have grown arrogant once more.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: It is no matter though, for your fate will be a reminder of what happens to those foolish enough to cross a Mahjarrat. Die now, knowing your efforts were in vain, for despite your best attempts, I have returned!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before you go any further, there's something we should discuss.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And why would I want to discuss anything with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because Ceril Carnillean is dead, and his killer may be trying to frame you!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Dead? How curious. Clivet, I trust this was not our work?

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: It was not, my lord.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Very well, adventurer. Speak your piece, but do so quickly. When you are done, I will decide if your life is forfeit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A member of the Khazard Army called Evelot was hired to steal a scroll from the Carnillean Mansion and deliver it here.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And she was succesful. Thanks to her, my followers were able to use that scroll to bring about my restoration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well during her escape, Ceril Carnillean was killed, but not by her. Someone else was there, but she doesn't know who.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The evidence I've found so far suggests that the killer was trying to frame her. By extension, they might have been trying to frame you as well.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: You suggest that this individual is my foe, but if that were true, why would they have arranged to have my restoration scroll delivered here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Whoever this person is, they must be an ally to us, my lord.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And yet, an ally who hides in darkness is no true ally at all.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, you say this Evelot is part of the Khazard Army?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Then I think it's time to make my return known to my Mahjarrat kin. Clivet, summon Khazard. Let us see what he has to say of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... is now a bad time to mention that Khazard and I have a... complicated history?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Well then, I suggest you prepare yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh boy...

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Vanar! Septinar! Filinar!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Hazeel? You've returned?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Surprised, Khazard? Or just disappointed?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Disappointed? I have no reason to be disappointed.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And yet you made no attempt to aid me yourself. Too busy claiming my lands as your own, perhaps?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: They were our lands! And... hang on... what is this human filth doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Filth? You're one to talk!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: It's clear that I've allowed you to endure for far too long! Allow me to correct that mistake.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Enough! This human is no friend of mine either, but [he/she] has presented me with information regarding my return that warrants investigation.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Then kill [him/her] and let's investigate. Or has your sleep made you go soft?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Make no mistake, if the human crosses me, I will gouge out [his/her] eyes and watch as [he/she] dines on them. Until then, [he/she] may be of use.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Fine, but I hope you know this is a foolish idea. What is the information [he/she] presented?

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Welcome, adventurer. It's good to see you once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alomone, I need to speak with Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: I'm afraid that isn't possible. Lord Hazeel is currently busy with his glorious work in the north. It is not our place to disturb him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it's very important. Has Jones not told you what's happened?

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Jones? We have not heard from him in some time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I guess I'll be the one to break the news instead. Ceril Carnillean has been murdered.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: He has? What joyous news! Long have we waited for those Carnillean monsters to face the consequences of their crimes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd hold off on the celebrating if I were you. Something strange is going on and I really need to talk to Hazeel about it.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Well... I suppose you have proven yourself to be a loyal servant to Hazeel. Still, I must warn you, our lord won't take kindly to being summoned unnecessarily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Very well. I will call upon the mighty Hazeel.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Vanar! Maradar! Dominar!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, you have summoned me. I trust you understand that my work in the north is of the upmost urgency. I have little time for distractions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assure you, this is important.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Then speak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A member of the Khazard Army called Evelot was hired to steal your restoration scroll from the Carnillean Mansion. She was meant to deliver it here, but of course, it had already been taken.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: During her escape, Ceril Carnillean was killed, but not by her. Someone else was there, but she doesn't know who.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Curious. Alomone, I trust this was not our work?

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Not to my knowledge, my lord. Your loyal servant, Jones, was still stationed at the mansion, but he would not have acted without instruction.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Where is Jones now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He fled after the murder took place. The guards are still searching for him.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The actions of a coward. He will come to regret that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something else as well. The evidence I've found so far suggests that the killer was trying to frame Evelot.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And you believe they were attempting to frame my followers and I as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I'm wondering.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Forgive me, my lord, but if they aimed to frame us, why would they also try to have your restoration scroll delivered here?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: A question with many possible answers, my faithful servant.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, you say this Evelot is part of the Khazard Army?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Then let us see what my Mahjarrat kin has to say about this. Alomone, summon Khazard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... is now a bad time to mention that Khazard and I have a... complicated history?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Well then, I suggest you prepare yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh boy...

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Vanar! Septinar! Filinar!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Hazeel? What is the meaning of this? And what is this human filth doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Filth? You're one to talk!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: It's clear that I've allowed you to endure for far too long! Allow me to correct that mistake.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Enough! I care not for your petty disagreements. This adventurer is under my protection. You are not to harm [him/her].

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Has the once great Hazeel gone soft? Never would I have expected you to work with scum like this. I trust you know that [he'll/she'll] betray you?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And if [he/she] does, I will gouge out [his/her] eyes and watch as [he/she] dines on them. Until then, cease this prattle.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Fine, but I hope you have good reason for this.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Someone has murdered Ceril Carnillean and attempted to pass the blame onto one of your minions. Were you aware of this?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: I don't involve myself in the personal affairs of my soldiers. Besides, I'd have thought you'd be happy to see that fool dead.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: I shed no tears for his demise, and if his killer turns out to be a friend, I will reward them with riches beyond imagination. However, this individual and their motives remain unknown. We must rectify that.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Bah! This matter is beneath the both of us. Accept this for the gift it is and move on.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: No. Any individual involving themselves in our affairs, be they friend or foe, must be made known to us, especially as the ritual draws near.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ritual?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Keep your nose out!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, how thorough has the investigation into Ceril Carnillean's murder been?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd say it's been pretty thorough. I handled most of it myself.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Have the family been questioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... not by me.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Ah yes, very thorough.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: No stone can be left unturned. Adventurer, you must return to the Carnillean Mansion and uncover any information you might have missed.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: You're trusting [him/her] with this? Stop this madness and let me handle it instead.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: This is not up for debate. I have made my will clear. Return to us once you have something, adventurer. Not before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why should I help you?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: You came to me, not the other way around. From that, I would assume that you already have some sort of motive in finding this killer. I care not what that motive is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you worried about my loyalties?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Loyalty means very little to my kind. You opposed me before, but now you have a chance to rectify that mistake. Do so, and you will be rewarded. Oppose me again, and you will die.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Loyalty means very little to my kind. Still, without you, I might never have been awoken from my slumber.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Continue to support me and you will continue to be rewarded. The moment you oppose me, however, is the moment you die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you think of any possible suspects?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Thousands. To have lived as long as I is to have made many friends, and many enemies. Any one of them may be responsible. Without further information, it is pointless to speculate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this ritual you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: It is nothing you need to concern yourself with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The more information I have, the more help I can be.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: You can be of ample help by performing the task you've been assigned. This idle chatter achieves nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be on my way.

sections.title: Talking to Alomone after he summons Hazeel

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Adventurer, I have summoned the mighty Hazeel as you desired. You should speak with him at once.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel after he is summoned

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, you have summoned me. I trust you understand that my work in the north is of the upmost urgency. I have little time for distractions.

sections.title: Talking to Alomone after he summons Khazard

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Adventurer, you should speak with Lord Hazeel and General Khazard at once.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel or General Khazard after Khazard is summoned

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Hazeel? What is the meaning of this? And what is this human filth doing here?

sections.title: Talking to Clivet/Alomone

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Lord Hazeel has given you a task to perform. Go and get it done.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Lord Hazeel has given you a task to perform. You should go with haste.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel after he asks you to investigate the Carnillean Mansion

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, you have work to do. Go to the Carnillean Mansion and uncover any information you might have missed. Return to us once you have something. Not before.

sections.title: Talking to General Khazard

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you think of any possible suspects?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: None at all?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: No. Now go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You and Hazeel seem to have some history.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Continue to bother me and I'll make you history. Now go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Then go away.

sections.title: The Mysterious Benefactor
Talking to Henryeta Carnillean before talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'd like to ask you some questions about what happened.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Please, leave me. If you have questions, take them to the guard outside.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe Carnillean before talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'd like to ask you some questions about your dad.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I shouldn't talk to you. You might be one of the bad people. The guard said I should be careful of the bad people.

sections.title: Talking to Claus the Chef before talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'd like to ask you some questions.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: I don't know if I should be answering any questions. You should probably check with the guard first.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck at the Fight Arena?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. I found Evelot and was able to question her. It seems someone might be trying to frame her.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Interesting. So what do you plan to do now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to have another look around and ask some questions.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well you'll be pleased to know we were able to track down Jones. He's already admitted to being part of that cult. For that alone he'll be going away for a long time. However, he's staying silent on the murder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, with what I've discovered so far, I don't think he was involved. I suspect he just fled out of fear.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Really? He seems like the obvious suspect. If not him, then who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I'm here to find out. I'd like to have another look around and ask some questions.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Go right ahead. Lady Carnillean and Philipe are just inside, and you'll find Claus down in the kitchen.

sections.title: Talking to Henryeta Carnillean

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'd like to ask you some questions about what happened.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: You're the one my husband hired to deal with that cult. Some good that did!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but right now we don't even think the cult were responsible.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Who else could it be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I'm hoping to find out.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Fine. What do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When your husband was killed, did you see anything unusual?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: No. It's as I told the guard. There was a noise coming from Jones' old room. My husband went to take a look and I heard him shout something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear what he shouted?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: I didn't. I went to see what was happening, and I heard a scream. By the time I arrived, the only one there was my husband, lying on the floor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And there was nothing else in the room that seemed odd?

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: No, nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thank you for your help.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe Carnillean

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'd like to ask you some questions about your dad.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: What questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see any of what happened?

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: No! I was in bed. Now leave me alone! I don't want to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'm sorry to have bothered you.

sections.title: Talking to Claus the Chef
Talking to Claus the Chef again

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Hello. Did you find anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it. Could you describe exactly what you felt and saw again?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: There was this sudden cold feeling and when I turned around, I thought I saw a shadow. Surely it was just a trick of the light...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If it was anything important, I'll find it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'd like to ask you some questions.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: But I already spoke to the authorities earlier.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, but I'm hoping I can uncover anything they missed.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Okay, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were you doing at the time Ceril Carnillean was killed?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: I was here in the kitchen. Sir Ceril is an early riser, and he likes a good breakfast as soon as he wakes up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you didn't see anything unusual down here?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're absolutely sure? Not even the slightest thing out of place?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Well... no... not really. I guess there was the cold...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cold?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Yes... this sudden cold feeling. I was right next to the warm stove, so it was very odd. I thought it was just some wind... but maybe...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see anything when you felt this cold?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Well... I thought I saw this shadow in the room, just for a moment. Surely it was just a trick of the light though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I'm going to have a little look around down here, if that's okay?

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Go ahead.

sections.title: Searching the south-west cooking shelves
Inspecting the wall before entering the hidden room
Talking to Claus the Chef after visiting the secret room

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Hello. Did you find anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Potentially, but I still need to do some more investigating.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Well, good luck.

sections.title: Inspecting the wall after entering the hidden room
Picklocking the chest
Searching the chest after it is unlocked
Reading the scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... I wonder what Hazeel will make of this.

sections.title: Talking to the Carnillean guard after obtaining the dusty scroll

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with your investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a new lead that I'm exploring.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Excellent. I hope it goes well.

sections.title: Talking to Claus the Chef after obtaining the dusty scroll

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Hello. Did you find anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. Hopefully it can help me find the killer.

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Well, good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Clivet/Alomone after obtaining the dusty scroll

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Have you completed your task? Speak with Lord Hazeel and General Khazard if so.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Adventurer, is your task complete? You should speak with Lord Hazeel and General Khazard if so.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel or General Khazard after reading the dusty scroll

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The adventurer returns. I trust this means you have something of note for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a hidden room beneath the Carnillean Mansion. There was a chest inside with a scroll in it.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: A scroll?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. It had a sketch on it of some sort of crest. Well... half of one, at least.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Half a crest? Could that be...?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: This chest, was there nothing else inside?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, just the scroll.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The crest must have been in there.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: So that was their true target. But I thought it was lost... How did it get there?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The Carnilleans must have claimed it after my defeat. The fools would have had no idea of its importance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this crest?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Who would even want to steal that thing? One of our kin, perhaps?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Quite possibly, though there are others who would know of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you two going to keep talking in riddles, or are you going to tell me what's going on?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Something is still amiss in all of this.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: I agree, but our next move is clear. We must go north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I've discovered the ability to turn totally invisible.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: We should not waste any time. However, we must first deal with the human.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: I will handle it.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, your work is done. Leave us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But we haven't found the killer.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Khazard and I will deal with this matter now. As a reward for your work, consider your past transgressions against us forgiven. Now go.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Khazard and I will deal with this matter now. Once again, you have proven yourself a worthy ally to my followers and I. Now go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: A group of ice trolls have made their home near the fortress. I will join you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ice trolls? Do you mean the trolls at Weiss?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Why are you still here?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Hold on. Adventurer, you know of these trolls and their lands?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I'm quite good friends with them. You see, there's this troll called My Arm...

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Nobody cares! Be gone from our sight.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: If the human knows these ice trolls, [he/she] may still be of use to us.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Why? We can make short work of any trolls that disturb us. We have no need for dead weight.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Until we know more about who we are dealing with, we should avoid attracting unneeded attention.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Enough of this nonsense! If you want subtlety, we can use the Shadow Realm.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: There are others who dwell in the Shadow Realm. I would prefer they be kept unaware of our presence in the north.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Fine, but on your head be it if this goes wrong.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, it seems we have further need of you after all. Meet us outside this troll settlement. Don't make us wait.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Alomone, in our absence, I trust you can take care of that matter we spoke of.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: It will be done, my lord.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Good. Now, adventurer, it seems we have further need of you after all. Meet us outside this troll settlement. Don't make us wait.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel or General Khazard after they say your work is done

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, your work is done. Leave us.

sections.title: Talking to Clivet/Alomone after Hazeel and Khazard leave

lines.speaker: Clivet
lines.text: Lord Hazeel and General Khazard require you in the north. Meet with them at the troll settlement there. Now be gone.

lines.speaker: Alomone
lines.text: Adventurer, Lord Hazeel and General Khazard require your presence in the north. You should join with them at the troll settlement there.

sections.title: In the North
Talking to My Arm or Snowflake before contacting the Mahjarrat

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Thank you for getting our herb patches to work. Now Boulder can grow goutweed for my people, and they are very happy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm meant to be meeting some... friends... Have you seen them?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: I don't think so... The only newcomers we've had here recently are those two strange trolls outside. I hope they're not up to no good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... okay.

sections.title: Talking to Big Fish or Dead Wolf
Talking Big Fish or Dead Wolf again

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Adventurer, speak with the leader of these trolls and find out if anything of note has occurred here recently. That will determine our next move.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Big Fish
lines.text: Good, you're here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... what?

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf
lines.text: The idiot doesn't even know it's us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... Hazeel? Khazard? Why are you trolls?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Given the secrecy of our task here, we felt it appropriate to shapeshift into less conspicuous forms.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: We? This foolish idea was yours alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So what are we doing here?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Based on your discovery, it is likely our mysterious killer will have travelled here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To Weiss? What would they want with the trolls?

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: Ha! They will have no interest in trolls.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: This troll settlement is a relatively new addition to the area. It is what was here before that will be of interest to the killer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which is?

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: Which is none of your business. Now, can we get to work?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Indeed. Adventurer, while our disguises will work up to a point, I imagine any troll would soon see through them if we were to converse with them.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: I doubt that. Trolls are as dumb as they come.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Our first step here is to gather information. As you claim to be friends with these trolls, you will be well placed to do so.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Speak with their leader and find out if anything of note has occurred here recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to other trolls after being told to talk to the leaders

lines.speaker: [Troll]
lines.text: Hey, friend of Snowflake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen anything odd around here recently?

lines.speaker: [Troll]
lines.text: Odd? Don't think so. Maybe you ask Snowflake and My Arm though?

sections.title: Talking to Odd Stone after being told to talk to the leaders

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Hey, friend of Snowflake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen anything odd around here recently?

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Odd? Well my name Odd Stone. Got named after some odd stone thing I found in snowfields south of Weiss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An odd stone thing?

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Yeah, it were made of stone, and it were odd. Some weird bone-head human saw me trying to nibble it, an' he got really upset an' chased me away. He kinda scary.

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Anyway, my parents, they named me Odd Stone after that, and now we trolls not go anywhere near the place. Bone-head human was, like, really scary! And probably magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A magic person with a bone-head? Interesting... Has anything happened with this stone recently?

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: No. We trolls blocked all routes to snowfields from here, but we keep eye out just in case. No bone-head human out there in ages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Probably not what I'm after then. Seen anything else odd?

lines.speaker: Odd Stone
lines.text: Don't think so. Maybe you ask Snowflake and My Arm though?

sections.title: Talking to My Arm or Snowflake
Talking to My Arm or Snowflake again

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Thank you for getting our herb patches to work. Now Boulder can grow goutweed for my people, and they are very happy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen anything odd around here recently?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Did you find da weirdness down in mine?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't looked yet.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Goat Poo was investigating the cave-in down there when he says he saw a shadow. Could be worth a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what my... companions... think.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Thank you for getting our herb patches to work. Now Boulder can grow goutweed for my people, and they are very happy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen anything odd around here recently?

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Odd? Like what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure to be honest. Anything unusual I guess.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: Well there's two strange trolls outside who I'm sure I haven't seen before. I hope they're not up to no good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes... those two. I was speaking to them earlier. They're harmless... I hope.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: If you want unusual, Goat Poo says he see da weirdness down in mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weirdness? What kind of weirdness?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: He say he see da strange shadow.

lines.speaker: Snowflake
lines.text: There was the cave-in recently down there as well. That's what Goat Poo was investigating when he saw the shadow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. I think my... companions... might want to hear about this.

sections.title: Talking to Big Fish or Dead Wolf after hearing about the weirdness in the mine

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: We're blind here. My senses do nothing this close to the ritual marker.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Nor mine, but there is no alternative.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: We wouldn't have this problem if we were in the Shadow Realm!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you two on about now?

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: Nothing! Stop eavesdropping!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's hardly eavesdropping if you're having the conversation right in front of me.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Adventurer, did you discover anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, but before we go any further, I think it's time for some answers.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: You're in no place to make demands!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe I just won't tell you what I've learnt.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: You waste time we may not have, adventurer, but very well. Ask your questions, but do so quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm done asking questions. What now?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Now you tell us what you have discovered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's been a cave-in down in the mine. One of the trolls apparently saw a strange shadow near it.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: A shadow? Could just be troll superstition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The cook at the Carnillean Mansion also mentioned seeing a shadow. That doesn't feel like a coincidence.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: It sounds like we should investigate for ourselves. Adventurer, meet us down in this mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would the killer come here?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Before the God Wars, a settlement of the old empire was found here. The fortress to the east is all that remains on the surface, but there is much still hidden below.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: A great prison, sealed long ago. Half of the crest used to do so came into my possession, but it was seemingly taken by the Carnilleans after my defeat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the killer stole it from the Carnilleans? You think they mean to open the prison?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: That is our assumption.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the other half of the crest?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: It was held by one of our kin, but we haven't felt his presence for some time. It's possible the killer already has it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why would someone want to open this prison? What's inside?

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: You've been told enough, human. Finish your questions and let us get back to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this ritual you keep talking about?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: What do you know of our kind?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Mahjarrat? Not a lot to be honest.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: Then let's keep it that way. This question has no relevance to our purpose here, Hazeel. It doesn't warrant an answer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If it's not relevant, why do you two keep talking about it?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: It may be relevant, it may not be. It depends on who this killer is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well assuming it is relevant, what can you tell me about it?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: The ritual is used by our kind to ensure our survival, and the next one is fast approaching. If the killer is one of our kin, their actions may be a means of preparing for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Preparing how?

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: For the rest of us to live, one of us must die. Many will go to great lengths to ensure they are not the one who is sacrificed.

lines.speaker: Dead Wolf (General Khazard)
lines.text: That's enough! You've already shared too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to go.

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: We have work to do, adventurer. Don't be gone too long.

sections.title: Talking to Big Fish or Dead Wolf after they offer to answer questions

lines.speaker: Big Fish (Hazeel)
lines.text: Ask your questions, adventurer, but do so quickly.

sections.title: Entering the salt mine

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... did Hazeel and Khazard already go inside?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Is this it?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Then let's get to work. First, you can't deny this time that the human is of no further use to us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here, you know?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Again with this, Khazard? [He/She] has been of great benefit so far. As long as that continues, [he/she] will remain.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: And what if I disagree?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The decision is already made.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Says who? You? You don't command me! Do you think you're my lord? Or maybe you think you're my father?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: No, and let us thank Zamorak for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, stop arguing and listen! I heard something.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, let us investigate. Khazard, stay here and guard the entrance.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: No. I'll go with the human. You wait here.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the... you!

sections.title: Defeating the assassin
Talking to Khazard again

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Why are you here? Go and tell Hazeel what happened. Now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'm going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: He's gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But where? Did he teleport?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: No. Just some cheap parlour trick. He's likely still nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We should look for him.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: We should, but first, you have some explaining to do. You knew him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well 'knew' is a bit of a strong word, but we've crossed paths.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Explain, and do so quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I encountered him on Entrana. He killed some monks there and stole a relic from them. I... may have been responsible for helping him, but I didn't realise until it was too late.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Ah, so you were always a fool?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're one to talk. So what was he doing here?

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: And there you go aptly proving my point. As it seems I have to explain everything, this door leads to the prison Hazeel spoke of earlier.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Your friend is likely the culprit in our little murder mystery, or at least, is working with the one responsible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it didn't look like he could open the door. He must still only have half the crest.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: And let us hope it stays that way. Now, I will stay here and guard the door in case your friend returns. Go and tell Hazeel what happened. Now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A 'please' wouldn't do any harm.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: 'Please' shut up and go.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel after defeating the assassin
Talking to Hazeel again

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, we must find the assassin. Search the rest of this dungeon and see what you can find.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Where is Khazard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's back there. There's been a development.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: What development?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We found the door to the prison, and we found someone trying to open it.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Someone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An assassin. I've encountered them before, but I don't really know anything about them.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And the crest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems they still only have half of it.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Curious. Why would they come here with only half the crest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps they didn't know they'd need the other half?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Possible, but unlikely. It should be clear to anyone that both halves are needed. I wonder...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: As I mentioned earlier, the other half was held by one of my kin. My kind have the ability to sense each other, but we have not felt his presence for a long time now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he's dead?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: That was my initial assumption, but it is unusual that we have not felt his passing either. It's possible he is masking his presence instead.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: It's no easy feat, but it can be done. As fate would have it, we stand in a place where our senses mean nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you think he's nearby?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: While foolish to do so, he would have good reason to hide himself in this area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the other half might be closer than we think. What do we do?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: This assassin must be found. I will remain here and ensure no one enters or leaves. You will search the rest of this dungeon and see what you can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would this other Mahjarrat hide himself here?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Our rejuvenation ritual takes place right above us. There have been times in the past when the weaker of our number have tried to hide themselves nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: To claim power from the ritual without the risk of being chosen as the sacrifice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I'm guessing this other Mahjarrat is quite a weak one then?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: You are correct. When the Empty Lord fell, he was one of the few foolish enough to remain loyal. His decision not to join Lord Zamorak left him with few friends. He has slowly grown weak ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's inside the prison that the assassin would want?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The prison was sealed long ago by two of my traitorous kin. They did so to prevent a great horror from escaping, but it is believed by some that a powerful artefact was also sealed inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the assassin wants this artefact?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Most likely. However, his attempts to claim it will risk unleashing what lies within.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which you consider to be a problem?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: In an ideal world, I would see this artefact claimed in the name of Zamorak. But to risk unleashing an abomination of the Fallen Empire when the ritual is so close would not be wise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the history between you and Khazard?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: That is none of your business. I have been generous enough to share a great deal of information with you so far, but I will not allow you to pry into personal matters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. Could you at least tell him to leave me alone? I feel like every time I turn my back, he's going to put his sword through it.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And have I not already been stopping him from doing so? Besides, his anger is not without cause. You wronged him, did you not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In my defence, he was doing some pretty nasty things. Not to mention, I also wronged you and you're not trying to have me killed constantly.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Yes, but you would be wise not to mistake my mercy for weakness. I am willing to forgive you for your past transgressions as I believe you can be of use.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: However, if I come to regret my choice, your fate will make the fires of Infernus seem welcoming in comparison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In my defence, he was doing some pretty nasty things.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: If you do not have the stomach for certain... unpleasantries, you should not have allied yourself with me, either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem more reasonable than Khazard, though.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Perhaps, but I trust you do not mistake that for weakness. If you wrong me, your fate will make the fires of Infernus seem welcoming in comparison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going.

sections.title: Talking to Khazard after reporting to Hazeel

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: No. I assume Hazeel has given you a job to do? Go get it done and leave me in peace.

sections.title: More Mahjarrat, More Problems
Upon entering the strange creature room

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor...!

sections.title: Leaving the strange creature room during the fight
Talking to Hazeel after encountering the strange creature

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, have you found the assassin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but I have found something... It's sort of hard to explain. I think I need to get back there.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Then go at once.

sections.title: Defeating the creature

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: Aargh... Muspah! Wait... What...?

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: Hmm... just another nightmare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just a nightmare? That feels like a bit of an understatement.

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: What is this? A human?

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: Who are you, human? Speak now, and do so with haste, for you stand before the mighty Jhallan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm [player name], and I'm guessing you're the Mahjarrat I'm looking for.

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: A human, seeking a Mahjarrat? Why? What would a meaningless being like you want with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have half a crest, right? There's an assassin who's after it. I'm trying to stop him.

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: You know of the Ghorrock Crest? How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a long story.

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: A story of lies, no doubt. I don't know how you came to stand before me, human, but you are clearly no friend of mine. Leave now, before I bring down the might of the Ruinous Powers on you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! It's true! This assassin is nearby. He'll be coming for you!

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: Then let him come! I am a faithful servant of the Empty Lord. I will not be intimidated by a pathetic human like you.

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: And who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, this is not good...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Khazard... we have a big problem!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: What have you done now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Jhallan, but the assassin captured him!

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Jhallan was here? Great. Hazeel!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The assassin captured Jhallan!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Then he now has everything he needs to enter the prison.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: What's our next...

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: Hang on...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Soooo...

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: What a mess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand. I didn't even see anything.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: It seems your friend has quite the range of abilities. He used the Shadow Realm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he got inside? Do we follow him?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: No good will come out of going in there. We have failed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we're not going to do anything?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Guards will need positioning here to warn us if this assassin remerges. Until then, we can only hope that he meets a swift end in the prison.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: I can have a detachment of soldiers despatched here at once. We'll need to keep things light to ensure the trolls don't notice our work. Not to mention our kin...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about Jhallan?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Still a prisoner of this assassin, no doubt. To what end, who can say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there really nothing else we can do?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: No. Word will be sent to you if there are any developments, but for now, you should leave. I suggest you return to the authorities in Ardougne and inform them that their killer will not be found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there's still so many unanswered questions!

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: And perhaps one day they will be answered, but to dwell on them now achieves nothing.

lines.speaker: General Khazard
lines.text: We should get to work.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Agreed. Adventurer, we are done here. If you wish, I can provide you with a teleport to Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll make my own way.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Very well.

sections.title: Entering the Crevice after defeating the strange creature

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: And who are you?

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel or General Khazard after the strange barrier disappears

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Soooo...

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel or General Khazard after declining the teleport

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, we are done here. If you wish, I can provide you with a teleport to Ardougne.

sections.title: Talking to Hazeel or General Khazard after accepting the teleport

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Adventurer, we are done here. You should return to Ardougne.

sections.title: Talking to the guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You're back. Any luck with your investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish I had better news, but I'm afraid I reached a dead end.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Why? What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The killer was an assassin. I don't know his true identity, but I previously encountered him on Entrana. He killed some monks there and stole an important relic.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: An assassin? Why would an assassin want Sir Ceril dead?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quite a bit is still unclear, but it seems he cared less about killing Ceril and more about recovering something hidden in the mansion.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Where is this assassin now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somewhere we have no hope of reaching him. Hopefully that changes, but for now, there's not much we can do.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well I guess I'd better go and file my report on all this. My superiors aren't going to be happy.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Still, thank you for all your help. Even though we weren't successful, I very much appreciate you trying. If you discover anything new about this assassin, do let me know.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well I guess I'd better go and file my report on all this. My superiors aren't going to be happy. First the Jones situation, now this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jones situation?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Ah, of course, you don't know. Jones was found dead in his cell. Poison by the looks of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes, quite the mess. Assumption is the cult are to blame. Tying up loose ends and all that.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Anyway, thank you for all your help. Even though we weren't successful, I still very much appreciate it. If you discover anythiing new about this assassin, do let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'll let you be on your way. All the best out there.

sections.title: Post-quest
Talking to the guard post-quest

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. How are things here?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Things aren't easy for the family, especially since they never got closure. They're getting through it, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to hear. Hopefully there's still a chance that the assassin is found one day.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: We can hope. If you do ever hear any more about him, be sure to let us know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. Now, I should leave you to your work.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Until next time.

sections.title: Talking to Philipe Carnillean post-quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Philipe Carnillean
lines.text: I don't want to talk to you.

sections.title: Talking to Henryeta Carnillean post-quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Henryeta Carnillean
lines.text: Please, do not disturb me. The loss of my husband is a hard burden to bear. I would prefer not to have distractions.

sections.title: Talking to Claus the Chef post-quest

lines.speaker: Claus the Chef
lines.text: Things aren't the same here now that Sir Ceril is gone.
